# GitWand

> GitWand is a free, open-source (MIT) native Git client that automatically resolves trivial merge conflicts using 8 deterministic patterns — no LLM guessing, no hallucinations. It auto-resolves ~95% of trivial conflicts with a confidence score and a full decision trace for every hunk. Built with Tauri 2 + Rust (~8 MB binary). Available as a desktop app (macOS, Linux, Windows), a CLI, a VS Code extension, and an MCP server for AI agents.

Key facts:
- Deterministic conflict-resolution engine: 8 auto-applied patterns (12-pattern registry), confidence scoring (certain/high/medium/low), decision trace per hunk. LLM fallback is strictly opt-in.
- Full Git client: changes, history (Git Tree), branches, push/pull, interactive rebase, worktrees, submodules, integrated PR review (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps).
- AI-agent friendly: MCP server (`@gitwand/mcp`), agent sessions (Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Gemini), scratch worktrees for AI tasks.
- Native performance: Tauri 2 + Rust, ~8 MB binary, local-first, zero mandatory cloud.

## Docs

- [Getting started](https://gitwand.app/guide/getting-started): install and first conflict resolution
- [Desktop app](https://gitwand.app/guide/desktop): the full Git client
- [CLI](https://gitwand.app/guide/cli): `@gitwand/cli` usage
- [MCP server](https://gitwand.app/guide/mcp): expose the engine to Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf and any MCP client
- [VS Code extension](https://gitwand.app/guide/vscode): resolve conflicts inside VS Code
- [Conflict resolution](https://gitwand.app/guide/conflict-resolution): how the patterns work
- [AI integrations](https://gitwand.app/guide/ai): agent sessions and providers
- [LLM fallback](https://gitwand.app/guide/llm-fallback): the opt-in LLM path and its audit trail

## Reference

- [Core API](https://gitwand.app/reference/core-api): `@gitwand/core` TypeScript API
- [Configuration](https://gitwand.app/reference/config): `.gitwandrc` options
- [CLI commands](https://gitwand.app/reference/cli-commands): full command reference

## Product

- [Features](https://gitwand.app/features): feature overview
- [Conflict engine](https://gitwand.app/conflict-engine): the resolution engine in depth
- [AI & agents](https://gitwand.app/ai-agents): MCP, agent sessions, AI features
- [Changelog](https://gitwand.app/changelog): full release history
- [Blog](https://gitwand.app/blog/): engineering and product articles

## Install

- Desktop: https://github.com/devlint/GitWand/releases (macOS .dmg, Linux .deb/.AppImage/.rpm, Windows .exe/.msi)
- Windows via winget: `winget install Devlint.GitWand`
- CLI: `npm install -g @gitwand/cli`
- MCP server: `npx @gitwand/mcp` — server card at https://gitwand.app/.well-known/mcp/server-card.json
- VS Code: search "GitWand" on the Marketplace
- Source: https://github.com/devlint/GitWand (MIT)
- Sponsor: https://github.com/sponsors/devlint

## Optional

- [Why I built another Git client](https://gitwand.app/blog/why-i-built-another-git-client): origin story
- [The state of merge conflict resolution in 2026](https://gitwand.app/blog/state-of-merge-conflict-resolution-2026): market overview
- [Automatic merge conflict resolution](https://gitwand.app/blog/automatic-merge-conflict-resolution): the engine's approach
- [Why we made LLM resolution opt-in](https://gitwand.app/blog/v2-5-llm-fallback): design philosophy
